SEARCH
Package neqsim.process.dynamics

Class AdaptiveRK45Integrator

java.lang.Object
neqsim.process.dynamics.AdaptiveRK45Integrator
All Implemented Interfaces:
Serializable, IntegratorStrategy
public class AdaptiveRK45Integrator extends Object implements IntegratorStrategy
Adaptive Runge–Kutta–Fehlberg (Cash–Karp) integrator producing a 5th-order solution with an embedded 4th-order error estimate. Step size is internally subdivided until the estimated local error meets the configured tolerance, then the high-order solution is returned.

The integrator preserves the IntegratorStrategy contract: callers ask for a single step of size dt and get back the next state. Internally the requested dt may be split into one or more sub-steps to keep the per-step error below absTol + relTol · max(|state|, |next|). The number of sub-steps actually taken on the most recent call is exposed via getLastSubSteps(), which is useful for diagnostics and for choosing better outer step sizes.

This integrator is well suited for stiff-but-not-fully-stiff dynamic studies where local behaviour varies sharply (compressor surge transient, fast valve closure, depressurization with choked-to-subsonic transition). For deeply stiff thermal/inventory dynamics prefer BDFIntegrator.

Version:
1.0
Author:
Even Solbraa

  • Constructor Details

    • AdaptiveRK45Integrator

      public AdaptiveRK45Integrator()
      Default constructor; absTol 1e-6, relTol 1e-4, maxSubSteps 1000.

    • AdaptiveRK45Integrator

      public AdaptiveRK45Integrator(double absTol, double relTol, int maxSubSteps)
      Constructor with custom tolerances.
      Parameters:
      absTol - absolute tolerance on local error (must be > 0)
      relTol - relative tolerance on local error (must be > 0)
      maxSubSteps - maximum number of internal sub-steps per outer step (must be >= 1)

  • Method Details

    • getName

      public String getName()
      Returns a short human-readable name (e.g. "Explicit Euler", "BDF-1").
      Specified by:
      getName in interface IntegratorStrategy
      Returns:
      integrator name

    • getAbsTol

      public double getAbsTol()
      Returns the absolute tolerance.
      Returns:
      absTol

      public double getRelTol()
      Returns the relative tolerance.
      Returns:
      relTol

    • getLastSubSteps

      public int getLastSubSteps()
      Returns the number of internal sub-steps consumed by the most recent step(double, double, neqsim.process.dynamics.IntegratorStrategy.Slope, double) call.
      Returns:
      sub-step count

    • getAbsoluteTolerance

      public double getAbsoluteTolerance()
      Alias for getAbsTol() for consistency with other libraries.
      Returns:
      absolute tolerance

    • setAbsoluteTolerance

      public AdaptiveRK45Integrator setAbsoluteTolerance(double value)
      Sets the absolute local-error tolerance.
      Parameters:
      value - must be > 0
      Returns:
      this integrator for chaining

    • setRelativeTolerance

      public AdaptiveRK45Integrator setRelativeTolerance(double value)
      Sets the relative local-error tolerance.
      Parameters:
      value - must be > 0
      Returns:
      this integrator for chaining

    • setMaxSubSteps

      public AdaptiveRK45Integrator setMaxSubSteps(int value)
      Sets the maximum number of internal sub-steps the adaptive controller may take inside one outer step(double, double, neqsim.process.dynamics.IntegratorStrategy.Slope, double) call.
      Parameters:
      value - must be >= 1
      Returns:
      this integrator for chaining

    • step

      public double step(double time, double state, IntegratorStrategy.Slope slope, double dt)
      Advances the state from x at time t by step size dt.
      Specified by:
      step in interface IntegratorStrategy
      Parameters:
      time - current simulation time in seconds
      state - current state value
      slope - slope function
      dt - step size in seconds (must be > 0)
      Returns:
      state at time t + dt
